<!-- docgen:version=0.1.0 reason: first generation of docs/ README for the claude-code-toolkit -->

## Purpose
Holds the user-facing safety documentation for the toolkit — specifically the audit checklist a user (or Claude on their behalf) runs before installing any hook, daemon, or scrubber. This is the "verify the clone, don't trust the maintainer" gate referenced by the parent README's Stage B.

## Files
- `SAFETY.md` — defines the toolkit's threat model (exfiltration, code execution on file contents, hidden install behavior, write overreach) and gives 5 concrete `grep`/`cat` audit commands with expected results; tells Claude when to refuse the install.

## Dependencies / collaborators
- `../README.md` — the parent README's "Stage B — Safety Audit" step instructs Claude to read `SAFETY.md` first; keep wording aligned.
- `../../secret-scrub/` and `../../docgen/` — the audit greps target these paths; if a new tool is added to the toolkit, its source paths must be added to the grep commands here.
- `../install.sh` and each sub-tool's `install.sh` / `install-launchd.sh` / `install-push-hook.sh` — audit check #4 expects each installer to remain small and to only touch declared paths.

## Gotchas
- The audit `grep` commands hardcode paths (`tools/secret-scrub/`, `tools/docgen/`) — if directory layout changes or a new tool is added, the checklist will silently pass by scanning nothing. Update the grep targets in lockstep with any structural change.
- Expected-write-path list in check #3 is the contract: introducing a new write destination (e.g. a new state file, a new hook location) requires updating this doc, otherwise an honest audit will flag the new tool as suspicious.
